## Tech Stack
A macOS-only, Apple Silicon dotfiles environment deployed via GNU Stow across 22 stowable packages. The primary languages are Zsh/Bash (shell config and automation scripts), Lua (Neovim plugins via lazy.nvim), and TOML (AeroSpace, Starship). TypeScript utility scripts run under Bun with no npm dependencies; a small Astro/React docs site lives under site/. Key tools include Neovim, Tmux, Ghostty, AeroSpace (tiling WM), Sketchybar (status bar), mise (polyglot version manager), BetterDisplay + displayplacer for external display management, and sleepwatcher for sleep/display-wake automation.

## Architecture
The repository is a collection of 22 modular GNU Stow packages, each a self-contained configuration unit whose internal directory tree mirrors its target location under $HOME. Deployment is fully idempotent: install.sh drives Homebrew, stow, mise, TPM, and lazy.nvim in sequence with existence guards at every step, then setup.sh renders LaunchAgent `.plist.template` files and symlinks Raycast script-commands. Shell automation scripts under scripts/scripts/ follow a consistent style (kebab-case names, set -u/-e, personal.env overrides, mkdir-based locking) and are executed either directly from .zshrc aliases, as launchd agents, or via Sketchybar plugins. A dedicated display-automation subsystem keeps the rig's layout + brightness stable: `com.lucas.sleepwatcher` runs `~/.wakeup` (bd-wake.sh) on both system wake (`-w`) and display wake/unlock (`-W`), which re-asserts the canonical layout via display-restore.sh (displayplacer) and brightness via bd-apply.sh (BetterDisplay CLI). Sketchybar is the integration hub connecting AeroSpace workspace events, BetterDisplay display modes, OBS, Spotify, system stats, and Claude Code billing. A Stream Deck profile is generated by streamdeck-build.ts (TypeScript/Bun) and fires Raycast script-commands (`raycast/script-commands/`, symlinked into Raycast's indexed dir) for both brightness modes and layout profiles. Neovim configuration is namespaced under lua/sethy/ with one-file-per-plugin specs; the environment is themed Catppuccin Mocha for terminals and Rose-pine for Neovim. A swiftc-compiled Swift KeepAlive LaunchAgent (`com.lucas.unlock-watch`, source at `scripts/scripts/unlock-watch.swift`, binary at `~/.local/bin/unlock-watch`) now owns post-unlock external-monitor recovery by observing `com.apple.screenIsUnlocked` via `DistributedNotificationCenter` and firing `~/.wakeup`; sleepwatcher's `-W` display-wake hook proved unreliable on Apple Silicon / macOS 26 and is no longer load-bearing for lock/unlock recovery.

## Conventions
- **file_organization** (conf 1): Each top-level directory is a GNU Stow package whose internal tree mirrors the target filesystem verbatim. No flattening or aliasing.
- **naming** (conf 0.95): All shell and TypeScript scripts use kebab-case filenames (bd-apply.sh, streamdeck-build.ts). No snake_case or camelCase script names.
- **naming** (conf 0.98): Shell functions use snake_case exclusively.
- **naming** (conf 0.92): Overridable per-rig env vars are prefixed DOTFILES_ in UPPER_SNAKE_CASE; internal vars UPPER_SNAKE_CASE without prefix.
- **other** (conf 0.9): Scripts accepting per-rig config source ~/.config/dotfiles/personal.env at the top with a file-existence guard, before any variable use.
- **error_handling** (conf 0.88): Bash scripts declare set -e / set -u immediately after the shebang. (Thin Raycast exec-wrappers are exempt — single exec, nothing to guard.)
- **other** (conf 0.93): Shell scripts use #!/usr/bin/env bash. Exception: launchd-invoked scripts (macOS stock /bin/bash 3.2) use #!/bin/bash and avoid bash 4+ features.
- **other** (conf 0.9): Pipe-delimited string tables (not associative arrays) for multi-column lookup data in scripts that may run under bash 3.2.
- **error_handling** (conf 0.95): Concurrent-access serialization uses an atomic mkdir mutex (flock absent on macOS), with a PID file for stale-lock reclaim and a timeout that proceeds rather than drops.
- **other** (conf 1): External tool inits in .zshrc are guarded with `command -v <tool>` before eval, preventing errors where the tool is absent.
- **file_organization** (conf 0.97): Neovim plugin configs each live in their own file under lua/sethy/plugins/ and return a single lazy.nvim spec table.
- **file_organization** (conf 1): Sketchybar config splits into items/ (declarations, sourced from sketchybarrc) and plugins/ (event scripts); colors/icons centralized.
- **other** (conf 0.92): TypeScript utility scripts use #!/usr/bin/env bun and prefer Node/Bun built-ins over npm; UI-asset generators define `const TOKENS = {...} as const`.
- **other** (conf 0.97): Install/setup scripts are fully idempotent — every action guarded by a prior existence check.
- **file_organization** (conf 0.9): Per-tool docs live in docs/<tool>/README.md; cross-cutting memory/decisions in MEMORY/*.md.
- **other** (conf 0.95): BetterDisplay CLI writes are guarded by readback verification with up to 3 retries before a WARN (the exit-0 lie of `betterdisplaycli set` makes readback mandatory).
- **styling** (conf 0.95): Catppuccin Mocha for terminal-facing tools; Rose-pine for Neovim; custom Durante dark palette for Sketchybar.
- **other** (conf 0.9): AeroSpace workspace-to-monitor assignments use quoted regex for external monitors and plain strings for the built-in, surviving monitor renames.
- **other** (conf 1, NEW 2026-06-22): LaunchAgent plists are stored as `.plist.template` files with a `__USER__` placeholder, rendered + bootstrapped by setup.sh's render_launchagents(); a repo-owned `com.lucas.*` agent supersedes any Homebrew brew-services equivalent so the flag set survives `brew upgrade`.
- **other** (conf 0.97, NEW 2026-06-22): Raycast script-commands delegate entirely to canonical scripts via `exec` (never re-implement); dotfiles-tracked wrappers in `raycast/script-commands/` are symlinked into Raycast's indexed dir by setup.sh's link_raycast_commands().
- **other** (conf 0.97, NEW 2026-06-24): Compiled native helpers (Swift sources in `scripts/scripts/*.swift`) are built to `~/.local/bin/` by setup.sh's build_native_helpers(), guarded by `command -v swiftc` (absent swiftc → silent skip, never error). Used only when launchd cannot express the required trigger as a plain plist (e.g. distributed notifications like `com.apple.screenIsUnlocked`).

## Key Decisions
- **GNU Stow is the sole deployment mechanism.** — Idempotent, reversible, package-granular symlinking; mirrored-path convention is the prerequisite.
- **Bun is the runtime for all TypeScript utility scripts, not Node.** — Faster one-shot startup; no npm deps / node_modules.
- **Oh-My-Zsh removed in favor of manual aliases + tool-native integrations.** — Saved ~200ms shell startup.
- **mise replaces fnm/pyenv/nvm.** — One eval vs three; unified per-project tooling declaration.
- **BetterDisplay mode-switching writes parameters directly via CLI, not favorite-mode slots.** — Slot-load broken in BD 4.3.0 pre-release; direct writes + readback are bulletproof (temporary workaround).
- **bash 3.2 compatibility for any launchd-invoked script.** — macOS /bin/bash is frozen at 3.2; use pipe tables, avoid bash 4+.
- **Per-rig config isolated to ~/.config/dotfiles/personal.env (untracked) with DOTFILES_ defaults.** — Keeps machine-specific values out of VCS while sharing one canonical script.
- **Display config re-asserted on display-wake, not just system-wake (NEW 2026-06-22).** — System sleep is prevented on this rig (`pmset -g` → sleep 0), so the displays sleep/lock far more often than the system does. A repo-owned `com.lucas.sleepwatcher` agent wires sleepwatcher's `-W` (display-wake) hook — not just `-w` (system-wake) — to bd-wake.sh, so layout + brightness reapply on every unlock. (ADR-008 — **superseded in part by ADR-009 (2026-06-24): `-W` proved unreliable in practice**.)
- **Unlock-triggered display recovery via `com.apple.screenIsUnlocked`, not sleepwatcher `-W` (NEW 2026-06-24).** — On Apple Silicon / macOS 26 sleepwatcher's `-W` registers but its IOKit display-wake notifications never fire, so bd-wake.sh never ran on unlock and the external monitor stayed dark. A swiftc-compiled Swift KeepAlive agent (`com.lucas.unlock-watch`) observes the reliable `com.apple.screenIsUnlocked` distributed notification and fires `~/.wakeup` on every unlock; sleepwatcher `-W` is reclassified best-effort, `-w` (system wake) stays authoritative. (ADR-009.)

## Setup
- Install: `/bin/bash install.sh`
- Dev: `zsh -i`
- Build: `bun run scripts/scripts/streamdeck-build.ts <src> <dst>` (Stream Deck profile)
- Test: `# no automated suite — verify manually`
- Lint: `# CI: .github/workflows/lint.yml`

## Changes Since Last Scan
Prior baseline: 2026-06-22.
- **New:** 1 convention (compiled-native-helper pattern), ADR-009 + 1 decision (`com.lucas.unlock-watch` on `com.apple.screenIsUnlocked`), the `com.lucas.unlock-watch` LaunchAgent + `scripts/scripts/unlock-watch.swift`, and setup.sh `build_native_helpers()`. Also a `.zshrc` compinit-ordering fix (compinit moved before the zoxide/fzf `compdef` callers).
- **Drifted:** 1 — ADR-008's `-W` (display-wake) reclassified non-load-bearing (proved unreliable on Apple Silicon / macOS 26).
- **Resolved:** 1 — external-monitor-dark-on-unlock gap closed by `com.lucas.unlock-watch`.
